title: Constructors and initialization

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific C++: Beginner to Intermediate draft for Constructors and initialization; human standards, technical, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: C++ Core Guidelines; living guidelines accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Constructors and initialization
sources.claim: Construction establishes a class invariant through member initialization in declaration order, and destruction releases owned resources after the object's lifetime ends.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Construction establishes a class invariant through member initialization in declaration order, and destruction releases owned resources after the object's lifetime ends.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A Fraction constructor validates a nonzero denominator and initializes normalized members directly in its initializer list. Your goal is to implement constructors without uninitialized members or unintended conversions. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Value, reference and const-reference parameters; pf data records; Class interface and invariants. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language rule and operational model
sections.body: Construction establishes a class invariant through member initialization in declaration order, and destruction releases owned resources after the object's lifetime ends. This rule is applied to concrete C++ source and observable behavior, not treated as a vocabulary definition alone.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Member order ignores initializer-list order, virtual dispatch is restricted during construction and destruction, and throwing constructors destroy only fully constructed subobjects. Therefore the draft explicitly rejects this beginner failure model: Members initialize in the order written in the constructor's initializer list.

includes: Initialize members in declaration order and distinguish default, converting and explicit construction.
Implement constructors without uninitialized members or unintended conversions.
excludes: Member order ignores initializer-list order, virtual dispatch is restricted during construction and destruction, and throwing constructors destroy only fully constructed subobjects.

outcomes: Implement constructors without uninitialized members or unintended conversions.

examples.title: Constructors and initialization: defined case
examples.situation: A Fraction constructor validates a nonzero denominator and initializes normalized members directly in its initializer list.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the concrete source or state: A Fraction constructor validates a nonzero denominator and initializes normalized members directly in its initializer list.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the conclusion: Members initialize in class declaration order; write initializer lists in that same order and establish the invariant before construction completes.
examples.result: The defined result follows because Members initialize in class declaration order; write initializer lists in that same order and establish the invariant before construction completes.

examples.title: Constructors and initialization: boundary case
examples.situation: A member declared before another is initialized first even when the constructor initializer list writes them in the opposite textual order.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Identify the changed source or state: A member declared before another is initialized first even when the constructor initializer list writes them in the opposite textual order.

Step 2 — Keep this standard boundary visible: Member order ignores initializer-list order, virtual dispatch is restricted during construction and destruction, and throwing constructors destroy only fully constructed subobjects.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting rule: Members initialize in the order written in the constructor's initializer list.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Implement constructors without uninitialized members or unintended conversions.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: Members initialize in the order written in the constructor's initializer list.
misconceptions.correction: Members initialize in class declaration order; write initializer lists in that same order and establish the invariant before construction completes.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Using concrete CPP rules, explain why this belief fails: Members initialize in the order written in the constructor's initializer list.

analogy.example: A system of self-managing components can model Constructors and initialization: each component protects an invariant and owns resources through typed interfaces and lifetime.
usefulFor: Connecting types, value semantics, invariants, generic interfaces, ownership, and deterministic cleanup
limitations: C++ overload resolution, templates, value categories, object lifetime, and undefined behavior have no exact physical equivalent.
Member order ignores initializer-list order, virtual dispatch is restricted during construction and destruction, and throwing constructors destroy only fully constructed subobjects.
